symptoms.symptom_name: Acute unilateral calf swelling (one leg about twice the size of the other)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Deep vein thrombosis of the lower limb
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Unilateral (asymmetric) leg swelling is a core Wells criterion and a hallmark of DVT.
Cellulitis also swells a limb, but usually with a demarcated advancing erythematous edge.

symptoms.symptom_name: Deep dull aching calf pain, worse on weight-bearing

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Deep vein thrombosis of the lower limb
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Deep calf pain/tenderness along the venous distribution supports DVT but is non-specific.
Calf pain is shared, but a strain typically follows sudden exertion, not gradual onset.

symptoms.symptom_name: Erythema, warmth and tightness of the skin over the calf

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Deep vein thrombosis of the lower limb
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Warmth and erythema accompany the inflammatory response to deep-vein thrombus.
Redness and warmth are shared features — the key reason cellulitis is the principal mimic.

symptoms.symptom_name: Recent 14-hour long-haul flight (prolonged immobility) 3 days before onset

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Deep vein thrombosis of the lower limb
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Long-haul travel immobility is a well-established provoking risk factor and raises the Wells pre-test probability.

symptoms.symptom_name: No shortness of breath, chest pain or haemoptysis (pulmonary embolism screen negative)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of respiratory/pleuritic features makes current PE unlikely, but does not exclude subsequent embolisation — hence mandatory safety-net.

symptoms.symptom_name: SpO2 99% on home oximeter, heart rate ~86 on wearable

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Normal patient-obtainable oxygen saturation and heart rate are reassuring against significant PE at present; unverified home readings, so weighted cautiously.

symptoms.symptom_name: Calf-circumference discrepancy >3 cm (measured 4.5 cm: right 41 cm vs left 36.5 cm)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Deep vein thrombosis of the lower limb
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A calf-circumference difference >3 cm is a Wells criterion; a clinician measurement not obtainable via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Palpable tender venous cord along the deep posterior tibial vein

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Deep vein thrombosis of the lower limb
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A palpable thrombosed venous cord strongly supports DVT; requires clinician palpation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Homan's sign (calf pain on passive dorsiflexion)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Deep vein thrombosis of the lower limb
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Homan's sign is insensitive and non-specific; a supportive but low-value clinician maneuver, not obtainable via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Compression duplex ultrasound showing a non-compressible deep vein

investigation_details.expected_result: Non-compressible deep vein confirming acute DVT
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: The diagnosis is not confirmed and anticoagulation decisions cannot be finalised; missed DVT risks pulmonary embolism.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Deep vein thrombosis of the lower limb
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A non-compressible deep vein on compression ultrasonography is the confirmatory finding for DVT — pending in this case.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Deep vein thrombosis of the lower limb
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A low-grade fever can accompany DVT; a high fever would point more toward cellulitis.
Only a low-grade temperature (not a high fever) makes significant cellulitis less likely.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Deep vein thrombosis of the lower limb
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Unilateral swelling + deep calf pain + warmth/erythema + a strong immobility risk factor + a >3 cm circumference discrepancy together give a high Wells probability.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirmatory test for DVT; a high pre-test probability warrants same-day/urgent imaging.
Stratifies probability and drives the imaging/anticoagulation pathway; a clinical tool the in-person assessor applies.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Baseline bloods before anticoagulation (FBC, renal function, coagulation profile)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Required to safely initiate and dose a direct oral anticoagulant.
In a HIGH pre-test probability (high Wells) presentation, a D-dimer must NOT be used to rule out DVT — proceed directly to duplex ultrasound; a negative D-dimer here is falsely reassuring.
